passive.name: Rage Gene
passive.description: While in combat Gnar generates Rage. At maximum Rage his next ability will transform him into Mega Gnar, granting increased survivability and access to new spells.

wiki_detail.blurb: '''Gnar''' generates ''Rage'' while fighting. At maximum ''Rage'', his next ability will be ''{{cis|Mega Gnar}}'' as he transforms, losing ''Mini Gnar's'' passive effects in exchange for increased stats and new abilities.
wiki_detail.blurb2: ''Mega Gnar'' form lasts for some time before reverting to ''Mini Gnar'', afterwards he cannot generate ''Rage'' for some time.
wiki_detail.description: {{sbc|Innate - Rage Gene:}} {{ci|Gnar|Mini Gnar}} generates {{pp|4;7;11|1 to 11}} ''{{ai|Rage Gene|Gnar|Rage}}'' over 2 seconds upon dealing or receiving damage. Against non-champions, [[basic attack]]s and ''{{ai|Boomerang Throw|Gnar}}'' instantly generate{{ft|{{pp|4*0.125;7*0.125;11*0.125|1 to 11|round=false}} ''Rage''.|{{fd|12.5%}} of the amount.}} Against champions, ''{{ai|Boomerang Throw|Gnar}}'' instantly generates{{ft|{{pp|4*0.25;7*0.25;11*0.25|1 to 11|round=false}} ''Rage'',|25% of the amount,}}and basic attacks instantly generate{{ft|{{pp|4*0.5;7*0.5;11*0.5|1 to 11|round=false}}.|50% of the amount.}}''{{ai|Boomerang Throw|Gnar}}'' can only generate ''Rage'' once per cast, and grants ''Rage'' based on the first enemy hit. '''Gnar's''' ''Rage'' decays after being [[combat status|out of combat]] for 13 seconds.
wiki_detail.description2: At maximum ''Rage'', '''Gnar's''' abilities are switched to {{cis|Mega Gnar}} abilities for 4 seconds. Casting one of '''Mega Gnar's''' abilities will cause him to transform into {{ci|Mega Gnar}}, else he will transform automatically after the 4 seconds have elapsed.
wiki_detail.description3: '''Gnar's''' {{ci|Mega Gnar|Mega}} form lasts for 15 seconds, with his ''Rage'' bar depleting as an indication of remaining time. ''Rage cannot be generated while in this form.'' Afterwards, '''Gnar''' returns to {{ci|Gnar|Mini Gnar}}, retaining his {{sti|health|{{as|'''current''' percentage health}}}} and becoming too [[File:Rage Gene 4.png|20px]] ''tired'' to generate ''Rage'' for 15 seconds.
wiki_detail.description4: '''Gnar''' gains different stat bonuses based on whether he is in ''{{ai|Mini Gnar|Gnar|Mini}}'' or ''{{ai|Mega Gnar|Gnar|Mega}}'' form. Both of '''Gnar's''' forms share their ability cooldowns.
wiki_detail.notes:
* The ''Rage'' generated upon dealing or receiving damage occurs over 2 seconds with 4 ticks every {{fd|0.5}} seconds. It also does not stack but instead refreshes with subsequent applications (of dealing or receiving damage).
* The bonus rage generation on basic attacks counts as an {{tip|on-hit}} effect, and is triggered by {{ii|Runaan's Hurricane}} and {{ii|Guinsoo's Rageblade}}.
* In-game, the value on the ''Rage'' bar always displays rounded down to the nearest integer.
* {{cis|Mega Gnar}} gained health is not affected by [[healing]] modifiers such as {{tip|Grievous Wounds}} and those from [[item]]s and [[rune]]s.
* When returning to {{ci|Gnar|Mini Gnar}}, '''Gnar''' will only retain his current health percentage, not current health total.
** This is intentional to prevent '''Gnar''' from simply triggering {{ai|Rage Gene 3|Gnar|Rage Gene}} in order to heal himself over time.
* '''Gnar's''' abilities share {{sti|cooldowns}} across forms.
* The final stats, after {{ai|Rage Gene 3|Gnar|Rage Gene}} being applied, can be seen on '''Mini Gnar's''' and '''Mega Gnar's''' respective data template, in the [[Gnar|front page]].
** '''Mini Gnar''' gains {{as|{{pp|key=%|5.5+({{ccd|Gnar|as_lvl}}-{{ccd|Mega Gnar|as_lvl}})*(x-1)*(0.7025+0.0175*(x-1))|formula=5.5 base + {{#expr:{{ccd|Gnar|as_lvl}}-{{ccd|Mega Gnar|as_lvl}}}} growth}}|attack speed}} {{as|'''bonus''' attack speed}}.

Q.name: Boomerang Throw / Boulder Toss
Q.description:
Gnar throws a boomerang that damages and slows enemies it hits before returning to him. If he catches the boomerang its cooldown is reduced.

Mega Gnar instead throws a boulder that stops on the first unit hit, damaging and slowing everything nearby. It can then be picked up to reduce the cooldown.
Q.tooltip: Mini Gnar: Gnar throws a boomerang that deals {{ spell.gnarq:minitotaldamage }} physical damage and Slows by {{ spell.gnarq:slowamount*100 }}% for {{ spell.gnarq:slowduration }} seconds. The boomerang returns after hitting an enemy, dealing reduced damage to subsequent targets. Each enemy can only be hit once. Catching the boomerang reduces its Cooldown by {{ spell.gnarq:minicdrefund*100 }}%.{{ spellmodifierdescriptionappend }}

W.name: Hyper / Wallop
W.description:
Gnar's attacks and spells hype him up, dealing bonus damage and granting him Move Speed.

Mega Gnar is too enraged to be hyper and instead can rear up on his hind legs and smash down on the area in front of him, stunning enemies in an area.
W.tooltip: Mini Gnar Passive: Every third Attack or Ability on the same enemy deals an additional {{ spell.gnarw:minitotaldamage }} plus {{ spell.gnarw:minipercenthpdamage*100 }}% max Health magic damage and grants {{ spell.gnarr:rhypermovementspeedpercent }}% Move Speed decaying over {{ spell.gnarw:minihasteduration }} seconds.{{ spellmodifierdescriptionappend }}

E.name: Hop / Crunch
E.description:
Gnar leaps to a location and bounces off the head of any unit he lands on, traveling further.

Mega Gnar is too large to bounce and instead lands with earth-shattering force, dealing damage in an area around him.
E.tooltip: Mini Gnar: Gnar leaps, gaining {{ spell.gnare:minibas*100 }}% Attack Speed for {{ spell.gnare:miniasduration }} seconds. If Gnar lands on a unit he will bounce off it, traveling further. Bouncing off an enemy deals {{ spell.gnare:minitotaldamage }} physical damage and briefly Slows by {{ spell.gnare:movespeedmod*-100 }}%.{{ spellmodifierdescriptionappend }}

R.name: GNAR!
R.description: Mega Gnar throws everything around him in a chosen direction, dealing damage and slowing them.  Any enemy that hits a wall is stunned and takes bonus damage.
R.tooltip:
Mini Gnar Passive: Increase Hyper's Move Speed.

Mega Gnar: Gnar tosses nearby enemies, dealing {{ damage }} physical damage, Knocking Back, and Slowing them by {{ rslowpercent }}% for {{ rccduration }} seconds. Enemies that hit a wall instead take {{ walldamage }} physical damage and are Stunned.{{ spellmodifierdescriptionappend }}

wiki_detail.blurb2: {{sbc|Active:}} {{ci|Mega Gnar}} {{tip|airborne|throws}} enemies around him in the target direction, dealing {{as|physical damage}} and {{tip|slow|slowing}} them upon their landing.
wiki_detail.blurb3: Enemies that collide with terrain are instead {{tip|stun|stunned}} and take increased damage.
wiki_detail.description: {{sbc|{{ci|Gnar|Mini Gnar}} - Passive:}} ''{{ais|Hyper|Gnar}}'' {{sti|ms|{{as|'''bonus''' movement speed}}}} is increased.
wiki_detail.description2: {{sbc|{{ci|Mega Gnar}} - Active:}} '''Gnar''' thrusts in the target direction, {{tip|airborne|knocking away}} nearby enemies up to 590 units in that direction, though not through terrain, and afterwards dealing them {{as|physical damage}}, as well as {{tip|slow|slowing}} them by 45% after a {{fd|0.4}}-second delay for a duration.
wiki_detail.leveling2:
{{st|Physical Damage|{{ap|200 to 400}} {{as|(+ 50% '''bonus''' AD)}} {{as|(+ 100% AP)}}}}
{{st|Disable Duration|{{ap|1.25 to 1.75}}}}
wiki_detail.description3: Enemies that collide with terrain take {{ccs|50% increased damage|physical}} immediately and are {{tip|stun|stunned}} instantly instead of {{tip|slow|slowed}} after a delay.
